key_insight: Tensorion generalizes Muon's spectral-norm constrained descent from matrices to higher-order tensors by using a tensor norm ball whose linear minimization oracle is tractable via adaptive unfoldings.
engineering_relevance: It offers a drop-in tensor-aware optimizer for models with multilinear weights, aiming for more stable updates and better convergence than vectorized Adam-style methods in tensor-structured settings.
method: Formulates optimization as steepest descent over a tensor norm ball.
Chooses a tensor norm that both bounds the tensor spectral norm and keeps the LMO computable.
Solves the LMO by reducing it to operations on adaptively selected unfolding matrices; recovers Muon exactly for matrices.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on tensor-based computer vision tasks, where Tensorion showed improved convergence behavior and more stable gradient updates versus Adam-based and other tensor-aware baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and cost of adaptive unfolding selection, which may add implementation complexity and overhead.
paper_title: Tensorion: A Tensor-Aware Generalization of the Muon Optimizer
